title: Myanmar assures India that its territory will not be used against India
content: The two sides reviewed the prevailing security situation along the India-Myanmar border and reaffirmed the importance of maintaining peace, stability, and security along the border

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE | Orange alert issued in Delhi
content: Rain lashed parts of Delhi in the early hours of July 9, extending a wet spell in the national capital and bringing much-needed relief to residents from the recent humid conditions

title: World Thirukkural Conference held in Bengaluru on July 5
content: Around 60 researchers from across India, Sri Lanka, Singapore, Malaysia, Canada, and other countries presented their research papers on Thirukkural

title: PM Modi in Australia LIVE: PM invites Australian businesses to invest in India, seeks early conclusion of CECA
content: PM Modi made the remarks at the Australia-India CEOs Forum and the Economic Roadmap Business event in Melbourne, which he jointly addressed with his Australian counterpart, Anthony Albanese.

title: Six killed, three injured in MUV-truck collision in Karnataka’s Uttara Kannada
content: The deceased have been identified as the driver Sanjay Angadi (33), Basavaraj (48), Abhishek Eshwar (28), Akshay( 26), Abhishek(26), Manjunath Chulaki(32), all from Dharwad.

title: Jharkhand CID arrests prime accused in Bokaro forest land scam from Patna
content: He has been arrested in Patna. The CID is further investigating the case and will bring the accused to Jharkhand on transit remand, CID said in a statement

title: Nod for guidelines ensuring dignified natural death for terminally-ill patients in A.P.
content: The State government has taken a significant decision to provide relief to patients suffering from severe illness with no prospects of recovery

title: ICU beds shown available, patient turned away: Delhi HC orders surprise hospital audits
content: The court highlighted inconsistencies in the use of the NextGen e-Hospital Management Information System (HMIS), noting that while the patient had not been assigned a Unique Health Identification (UHID) at Jag Pravesh Chandra Hospital, she was assigned one at LNJP Hospital

title: PM Modi calls for deeper India-Australia strategic ties amid global uncertainty
content: Over the past few years, we have built a strong framework for future cooperation by combining the capabilities of both countries, said Prime Minister Narendra Modi

title: PM in Indonesia highlights: Modi addresses Indian diaspora, says country moving forward with mantra of 'reform, perform and transform'
content: PM Modi conferred with Indonesia's highest honour ’Bintang Adipurna of the Republic of Indonesia’ medal

title: BJP has destroyed the MGNREGA scheme: TNCC president Manickam Tagore
content: Tagore’s counterattack on BJP State chief Nainar Nagendran over the Mekedatu dam project and MGNREGA came shortly after Nagendran criticised him

title: SAD says will screen film ‘Satluj’ across Punjab
content: The film, which was earlier titled “Punjab ‘95” and details the life of activist Jaswant Singh Khalra in Punjab in the traumatic phase of militancy between the mid-1980s and early-1990s

title: Chandigarh police bust Pakistan -linked narco terror network after raids in border districts, 3 held
content: Chandigarh Police have also formally arrested the main conspirator of narco terror network, members of which were allegedly involved in the cashier's killing

title: CMDA plans to shelve projects as it grapples with outstanding liabilities up to ₹3,500 cr.
content: The liabilities are mainly due to the construction work, land acquisition, and the Vada Chennai Valarchi Thittam, which constitutes ₹1,000 crore. A source says that CMDA took up a lot of works without realising the financial burden and cannot take up new works in the present financial position

title: CM Vijay conducts surprise inspection at Govt. Hospital for Women and Children in Egmore
content: At the century-old institute, Mr. Vijay visited the ward and the intensive care unit and enquired about the health of mothers undergoing treatment at the hospital

title: There is a need to build a national front against BJP: Thirumavalavan
content: “There is a necessity to build a national anti-BJP front — both the TVK and DMK should be a part of it. This is my point of view...but many would say that I am confusing everyone”, Mr. Thirumavalavan said

title: Nearly 23% of Karnataka’s youth force under 35 is neither working nor studying: Report
content: A lack of or inadequate training with fewer opportunities for work is pushing the youth force to leave their homes and migrate to Bengaluru, straining the State capital, says ‘Karnataka State Handbook on Youth Opportunity’

title: Bengaluru Assembly segments lag in enumeration form distribution as Karnataka SIR reaches 82% coverage
content: Among the Assembly constituencies with lower distribution rates, Bommanahalli recorded the lowest progress at 35.01%, followed by Bangalore South (39.95%) and Govindarajanagar (40.25%)

title: Committed to attracting 500 new global capability centres to create 3.5 lakh jobs, says Karnataka Chief Minister
content: D.K. Shivakumar met over 200 GCC leaders and said his government’s aim is to generate $50 bn in economic output by 2029

title: Bishnoi, aides used WhatsApp from Gujarat jail to extort victims: U.S. DoJ
content: Echoing NIA’s 2022 chargesheet, DoJ says Bishnoi’s “enterprise routinely targeted prominent religious, social, and political leaders with violence”; his lawyer says “authorities should give supporting evidence” of gangster using messaging apps

title: 646 Assam govt. schools without electricity, 283 lack boys’ toilets
content: Education Minister Ranoj Pegu says the State seeks to provide all essential infrastructure by March 2027

title: Rajasthan Congress takes exception to public consultations on UCC without release of draft
content: Party demands complete draft of the Bill; says no feedback can be provided by the public without knowing what the proposed law contained; State forms high-level committee to draft legislation

title: Justice Basha Commission has sought more time to complete consultations on anti-honour killing law: Minister
content: The Commission was still receiving petitions and representations from victims through social activists and had also sought the views of political parties: Vanni Arasu

title: Police crackdown on suspected drug traffickers meets fierce resistance
content: Police say the cyber cell has identified several persons linked to inter-State and inter-district synthetic drug traffickers

title: V-C running varsity unilaterally, allege Kannur University Syndicate members
content: Syndicate members allege irregularities in academic appointments, question the appointment of a senior official, and demand greater transparency in the university’s administration

title: 129 shifted to relief camps after Wayanad debris slip
content: Ministers direct officials to strengthen relief and disaster management measures after the tragedy that claimed three lives, with five persons still missing

title: Congress appoints new in-charges for Tamil Nadu, West Bengal
content: Ghulam Ahmed Mir, who was earlier in charge of West Bengal, is the new AICC general secretary in-charge of Tamil Nadu while Prakash Joshi has been appointed in his place.

title: Ajay Chautala calls Haryana CM Saini a ‘dummy’ at Chanot protest site
content: JJP chief backs Chanot villagers’ protest over drinking water shortage as BJP government defends its pipeline plan

title: Floods, landslides affect several Arunachal districts
content: According to the SEOC, Upper Siang was the worst-hit district, followed by Siang and Kra-Daadi; Upper Siang recorded landslides at 16 locations

title: Delhi, Mumbai grapple with heavy rain havoc | Monsoon highlights
content: Three-storey under-construction house in Delhi's Rohini collapsed on Wednesday evening, trapping several people; 16 people are feared to be trapped under a collapsed garbage mound in Maharashtra's Pimpri Chinchwad

title: BJP lost credibility worldwide in wake of Ram Temple embezzlement case, says U.P. Opposition
content: SP claims global investors are withdrawing funds because of a loss of trust in the BJP government; Congress says if PM took credit for Ram Temple construction, he must also accept responsibility for embezzlement allegations

title: Donation racket alleged at Madhya Pradesh temple, probe ordered
content: Managed by a government committee, it’s alleged that a parallel, ‘unauthorised committee’ was operating in the temple premises, accepting cash and valuable ornaments, and issuing fake receipts; Congress slams the BJP

title: Kumarakom to get solar-powered tourist boat services
content: The initiative, expected to be launched from Muhamma, will enable tourists to travel to popular destinations such as Pathiramanal, Kayippuram and Thanneermukkam, among other

title: Narasaraopet to host ₹2,000 crore mega loan mela on July 17: MP
content: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman and Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu are expected to attend the programme, which aims to benefit about 3,000 people under around 20 Central government schemes

title: Wayanad debris slip highlights: CM announces ex gratia of ₹5 lakh to families of killed; says priority is to trace missing people
content: A day after a debris slip at the tunnel road construction site in Kerala's Wayanad district claimed three lives, extensive search operations are ongoing to find five people who are still missing

title: Amit Shah orders third-party audit to cut airport passenger delays
content: Shah directed the phased installation of Automatic Tray Retrieval System at airports across the country, to ease the congestion at security checkpoints and reduce manpower requirements

title: Supreme Court seeks government response on plea by Gulf students that ‘assessment scheme’ hurts prospects
content: Students say the assessment scheme ‘drastically lowered their marks and jeopardised’ their higher education prospects

title: PM Modi's Australia visit: Early trade pact, big investment, clean energy and more
content: India and Australia are strengthening economic ties and bilateral cooperation. Prime Minister Modi invited Australian businesses to invest in India's expanding economy. AustralianSuper announced a significant new investment in India's infrastructure fund. Discussions covered opportunities in clean energy, critical minerals, and technology sectors. Both nations aim to expand cooperation in trade and emerging sectors.

title: Who is Arnav Paparkar? Meet the first Indian in 36 years to reach Wimbledon quarterfinals
content: Young Indian tennis player Arnav Paparkar reached the Wimbledon boys' singles quarterfinals. He became the first Indian in 36 years to achieve this prestigious feat. Paparkar defeated Japan's Ryo Tabata in a dominant performance on Wednesday. This historic achievement places him among elite Indian tennis players. He will now face American qualifier Jordan Lee in the next round.

title: Love, affair, wife's death: How two civil services aspirants ended up in murder probe
content: Police arrested a sub-inspector in connection with the death of Amrita Kumari, wife of a block development officer. Her brother alleged her husband killed her due to an extramarital relationship and dowry harassment. The couple met while preparing for civil services and later married, but their relationship reportedly soured. Amrita was found unconscious and later died, leading to her brother filing an FIR against her husband and his family.

title: California-based tech company 'banned' for Chinese links
content: The Federal Communications Commission has officially placed Digitalsystem Technology on its national security risk list, consequently prohibiting the company from offering international telecommunications services. This decision arises from concerns about its affiliations with Chinese telecom firms and ownership stakes. The FCC highlighted the potential for exploitation by Chinese threat actors as a significant threat, emphasizing the determination of significant and unacceptable national security risks.

title: FIFA WC: Paraguay senator Amarilla launches fresh attack on Mbappe, refuses to back down
content: Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla intensified her verbal attacks on Kylian Mbappe.  She accused the French captain of disrespecting Paraguay's goalkeeper after their match.  Amarilla refused to apologise for her earlier racist and dehumanising remarks.  The controversy has drawn condemnation from French and international bodies.  Mbappe remains focused on the World Cup despite the ongoing off-field dispute.

title: New USCIS rules for H-1B, Green Card applicants start July 10
content: New United States Citizenship and Immigration Services rules will take effect soon. Applicants must use handwritten or properly reproduced signatures for immigration forms. Improper signatures will lead to rejection without any chance for correction. Applicants should always sign forms by hand before submitting them. This policy aims to maintain the integrity of the immigration system.

title: Big blow for Morocco ahead of France quarter-final as Ismael Saibari ruled out
content: Morocco's forward Ismael Saibari is out of the France quarter-final match due to a hamstring injury. Coach Mohamed Ouahbi confirmed the influential player's absence before the crucial game. Despite this setback, Ouahbi stated Morocco will maintain their established playing style against France. France enters the match with a perfect record and aims for a third consecutive semi-final appearance.

title: Connor Murphy's alleged last video saying 'I'm dying every second' resurfaces after his death
content: Popular American fitness influencer and YouTuber Connor Murphy has died at the age of 32 after a drowning incident in Thailand. In the wake of his death, a video widely shared on X as his "last video" has gone viral, but the clip has since been clarified as an old recording. The video showed Murphy speaking directly to the camera, saying he felt like he was "dying every second."

title: Parents take note, your phone addiction too can be a problem for children
content: Children may develop insecure attachment due to parental device distraction. This can negatively affect their confidence and relationships throughout life. Researchers highlight that parents' screen habits influence children's emotional well-being. Many adolescents feel ignored when parents are engrossed in their phones. This study examines the impact of parental technoference on young people.

title: Rajesh Sharma health update: AICWA seeks high-level probe into Prabhas' 'Fauji'
content: Veteran actor Rajesh Sharma suffered a medical emergency while filming Prabhas' movie 'Fauji'. The All Indian Cine Workers Association has demanded a high-level investigation into the incident. They are questioning the availability of proper medical facilities on the film set. AICWA seeks accountability and complete medical support for the actor's recovery. The association also wants producers to bear all treatment expenses if negligence is proven.

title: Muslim divorce pronounced privately can still be formally declared by court: Allahabad HC
content: A high court ruled family courts must declare divorce for undisputed private talaq. The court stated such declarations are within family court powers. A couple's marriage breakdown led to a husband's talaq pronouncement. The wife acknowledged receiving all talaq notices and did not dispute it. The high court set aside the family court's dismissal and decreed the divorce.

title: Chilling new images reveal condition of Connor Murphy’s Thailand home before drowning death
content: Connor Murphy died in Thailand after reportedly fleeing police and entering a lake, where he later drowned. Newly released images from his rental home show trash, medication packets and damage inside the property. Police are still investigating the case and awaiting autopsy and toxicology results. Authorities have not confirmed what led to Murphy’s behavior before his death.

title: NHL trade rumor: Penguins, Flyers eye Canucks' $92M star
content: The Vancouver Canucks have been actively looking for trade options for the team’s star center Elias Pettersson as the team rebuilds its roster, ahead of the new season. Elias Pettersson’s performance has also dropped in the last few years which has sparked some concern. Amid all the mess, it seems the Pittsburgh Penguins and the Philadelphia Flyers have emerged as potential landing spots for him.

title: Blake Lively didn't expect this from Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce: Report
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce just had the wedding of the year as 1000 high profile guests belonging to Hollywood and the sports world lined up to watch the pop star walk down the aisle, sparking chaos among milllions. While the wedding had brought New York City to a standstill, it seems Blake Lively might not have been that disappointed with not being invited.

title: 13 killed in Surat rains: Floodwaters continue to inundate homes, markets and roads
content: Large parts of Surat remained submerged on Wednesday after heavy rains eased. Stagnant water choked roads, homes, and commercial complexes, causing significant disruption. Overflowing creeks spilled into neighborhoods, leaving many areas inundated with waist-deep water. Transport networks suffered as buses and vehicles remained stranded in floodwaters. Rescue operations continued, and schools and industries remained shut due to the ongoing situation.

title: Champat Rai’s ‘letter’ to SIT blames Anil Mishra for mismanagement
content: Champat Rai claims Anil Mishra and SBI officials framed SOPs without his approval.  Mishra allegedly handled administrative and financial powers with SBI, leading to mismanagement.  The usual currency note handling codes may not have been followed by bank staff.  An agreement was made with SBI's Ayodhya branch chief during Maha Kumbh.  Rai stated all other correspondence with SBI was handled by him since August 2020.

title: Katie Couric reveals diagnosis of transient global amnesia
content: Katie Couric, a veteran in journalism, endured an unexpected bout of memory loss, prompting a diagnosis of transient global amnesia. This neurological condition induces a temporary struggle to create new memories, which can be daunting. Fortunately, TGA is often harmless, and individuals typically see a complete recovery within twenty-four hours after such incidents.

title: Cristiano Ronaldo's two-word message after Portugal's World Cup exit
content: Cristiano Ronaldo's FIFA World Cup journey concluded after Portugal's Round of 16 loss to Spain. He shared an emotional message on Instagram, stating "Portugal Forever" after the match. Ronaldo became the oldest player to appear in a World Cup knockout game at 41. He also set a record for most shots without creating a chance. This marked the end of his illustrious World Cup career.

title: Trump’s ceasefire ‘over’ remarks & fresh Strait of Hormuz disruptions: What it means for India
content: Before the conflict, the Strait of Hormuz handled nearly 40% of India's crude oil imports, around 60% of its LNG imports and approximately 90% of its LPG supplies, underlining the country's heavy dependence on the strategic waterway.

title: Watch: First AC coupe of express train turns into ‘honeymoon suite’; TTE suspended
content: A travelling ticket examiner was suspended after a train coupe was decorated for a couple.  The compartment was adorned with balloons and flowers, creating a honeymoon suite ambiance.  Authorities ordered an inquiry into the incident which occurred at Jalna railway station.  An event styling service decorated the coupe, and the decorator faced charges.  The railway views the decorator's entry as a serious unauthorized lapse.

title: Hometown hero Fery makes Wimbledon history as second wild card ever to reach semis
content: British wildcard Arthur Fery has reached the Wimbledon semifinals in a remarkable achievement. He defeated Flavio Cobolli to become only the second men's singles wildcard in history to do so. Fery's surprising run has earned the nickname "Ferytale" among fans and media. He will now face second seed Alexander Zverev in the next round.

title: Crude nears $80 a barrel again after Trump declares US-Iran ceasefire ‘over’
content: Global oil markets are experiencing renewed chaos as crude prices surged significantly. US President Donald Trump declared the ceasefire agreement with Iran was over. Fresh US military strikes on Iranian targets heightened fears of supply disruptions. Maritime authorities raised the threat level for vessels transiting the strait. The rise in oil prices renews concerns over inflation and potential interest rate hikes.

title: Delhi-NCR wakes up to another rainy morning; IMD issues orange alert
content: Delhi received heavy rainfall early Thursday, extending the monsoon spell and bringing relief. An orange alert forecasts more rain across the region through most of the day. This continuous rainfall caused waterlogging in several parts of the national capital. Similar weather is expected over Haryana, Chandigarh, and west Uttar Pradesh until July tenth. Northeast Uttar Pradesh will see rain until July thirteenth, and east Rajasthan on July ninth.

title: UPTET answer key released: Objection window open until July 14
content: UPTET Answer Key 2026: The Uttar Pradesh Education Service Selection Commission (UPESSC) has released the UPTET Provisional Answer Key 2026 and opened the online objection window until July 14. Candidates can download the answer key, verify their responses and estimate their scores. Objections must be submitted online with supporting documents, as offline requests will not be accepted. After reviewing all challenges, the commission will publish the final answer key, which will be used to prepare the UPTET Result 2026.

title: Bengal shocker: Man slits wife's throat on busy road, attempts suicide
content: A husband allegedly murdered his wife by slitting her throat in Sodepur. He then attempted suicide by stabbing himself and is in critical condition. The couple had been experiencing marital discord for some time. Geeta had recently left her matrimonial home and returned to Sodepur. Police are investigating the incident and reviewing CCTV footage.

title: 'It will get much worse': Trump as US launches fresh strikes over Iran's Hormuz 'aggression'
content: United States Central Command forces conducted new strikes against Iran on Thursday. These actions aim to weaken Iran's ability to threaten shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The strikes followed Iran's recent aggression against commercial vessels and civilian crews. President Donald Trump indicated the possibility of further swift military action against the Islamic Republic. This escalation follows exchanges of strikes on military targets and shipping incidents.

title: Ind vs Eng: 142 years after men's, Lord's hosts first-ever Women's Test
content: The England-India Test, beginning on Friday, will be the first women's Test at Lord's. It comes 142 years after the ground hosted its first men's Test.

title: Another paper leak? UGC-NET under lens
content: The National Testing Agency faces new allegations of a paper leak for the UGC-NET exam. This follows recent issues with the NEET-UG exam and errors in other NET papers. Education ministry seeks details on a circulated PDF allegedly linked to the sociology paper. Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi highlighted these serious allegations on social media. The controversy adds to existing concerns about high-stakes examination integrity.

title: Canada: Lawrence & gang directed Nijjar hit, there's nothing to link Indian govt
content: Canadian police found no evidence linking India's government to organized crime charges. These charges involve the killing of Hardeep Singh Nijjar in Canada. US indictments against Lawrence Bishnoi and others did not implicate Indian officials. This development supports India's stance on the 2023 allegations. Relations between India and Canada are improving after a period of strain.

title: 'Alpha' nears Rs 75 crore mark at box office on Day 6
content: Alpha continues its theatrical run, nearing the Rs 75 crore worldwide milestone. The action thriller earned an estimated Rs 2.85 crore net in India on Wednesday. Its India net collection reached Rs 44.95 crore and overseas gross Rs 21.30 crore. The film's worldwide gross collection now stands at Rs 74.89 crore globally. It aims to cross Rs 100 crore worldwide after the upcoming weekend.

title: ED freezes Rs 440 crore in three TMC accounts in laundering case
content: The Enforcement Directorate has frozen TMC bank accounts holding Rs 440 crore.  These funds are linked to an alleged Rs 160 crore money laundering investigation.  The money was reportedly used to purchase an aircraft and a helicopter.  These assets were then rented back to the TMC for party use.  The agency is probing dubious transactions and potential personal asset creation.

title: No fruit in fruit candy, no cocoa in chocolate: FSSAI cracks down on misleading labels
content: Food regulators have issued notices to several companies for misleading product labels.  Kubera Foods faced scrutiny for claiming "100% Natural" on buns with preservatives.  Ferns N Petals was cited for marketing chocolate with vegetable fat instead of cocoa butter.  Lotte India received notices for "100% Vegetarian" claims and missing nutritional information.  These actions aim to ensure consumers receive accurate information about food products.

title: Quote of the day by Peter Dinklage: 'Women are not as shallow as men are...'
content: Peter Dinklage gained global fame for his role as Tyrion Lannister. He largely keeps his private life away from the public eye. Dinklage discussed his evolving confidence and relationships with women. He believes women often see through superficial things more easily. Upcoming films for the celebrated actor include 'Idiots' and 'The Reckoner'.

title: Mahadev app founder Saurabh Chandrakar held in Oman
content: Mahadev betting app founder Sourabh Chandrakar has been detained in Oman. India is now seeking his extradition or deportation from the country. Chandrakar was previously detained in Dubai but was later released by authorities. The app operated as a large international betting syndicate facilitating illegal online bets. Authorities have attached assets worth Rs 1,700 crore linked to Chandrakar.

title: Woman nearly loses finger after door slams shut on her, doctors replant it within hours
content: A Mumbai woman's finger was successfully replanted after a severe door crush injury. Doctors performed complex microsurgery to restore circulation to the amputated digit. The patient had significant medical challenges including diabetes and tobacco use. This case highlights the advancements in modern microsurgery for limb salvage. Early referral and specialized treatment are crucial for successful replantation outcomes.

title: 'Begging For Deal': Trump Shares Video Of '20 Times' Bigger US Strikes On Iran
content: Iran state media reported explosions in multiple locations along the southern Iranian coast, including Bushehr, home to Iran's nuclear power plant complex, and the southern port cities of Chabahar, Konarak, Bandar Abbas, and Sirik. An airbase in Iranshahr also came under US military fire.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Markets To Open Higher Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude futures were up 78 cents at $78.80 a barrel, while US WTI crude is at $74.26.

title: Mahadev App Promoter Detained In Oman, India Seeks Extradition
content: India pursues extradition of Mahadev app founder Chandrakar detained in Oman over Rs 6,000 crore betting racket.

title: Iran War Flare-Up Rattles Markets. Should You Buy The Dip Or Wait It Out?
content: Strong forex reserves, resilient services exports, healthy remittances and a credible inflation-control framework have changed India's growth outlook.

title: US Conducts Fresh Strikes Against Iran, Cites Attacks On Ships
content: The latest round of attacks, which the United States said was launched in response to Tuesday's assault on three cargo ships transiting the strait, rattled several cities along Iran's southern coast and left some areas without power.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: 'Hard Slap Coming,' Says Iranian Lawmaker In New Threat To US
content: Iran-US War News LIVE: The US military has hit a variety of military sites and port facilities after blaming Iran for targeting several merchant vessels off the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Monsoon Spell Continues In Delhi, Waterlogging In Several Parts
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Heavy monsoon rain hits Delhi NCR with waterlogging reported in key areas

title: Iran Calls Trump "Criminal And Murderous" As US Ramps Up Attacks
content: Iran said the understanding between Tehran and Washington was never based on trust but on the principle of "commitment for commitment", alleging that the US had breached the agreement through unilateral actions.

title: Iran To Bury Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei Today In Hometown Amid US Strikes
content: Iranians were preparing on Thursday to bury their slain leader Ali Khamenei in his hometown of Mashhad in northeast Iran, as new US strikes threatened to trigger a fresh escalation in the Middle East war.

title: US Court Orders Trump To Pay Ex-Columnist $5 Million In Sexual Assault Case
content: A US judge ordered President Donald Trump on Wednesday to pay writer E. Jean Carroll $5 million after a civil jury found that he sexually assaulted and defamed her.

title: US Bombing To "Get Much Worse" If Iran Doesn't Halt Attacks, Warns Trump
content: President Donald Trump warned Wednesday that US bombing of Iran will increase significantly if Tehran continues to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: US Ramps Up Attacks On Iran Amid Ongoing Talks. What Next?
content: Iran War: Trump warned Wednesday that a new round of US attacks was coming, even as he attempted to shrug off suggestions of a return to full-scale war.

title: Keir Starmer Claims Turkey's Erdogan Gifted Pistols To Fellow NATO Leaders
content: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan offered a pistol as a gift to each of his fellow leaders who attended this week's NATO summit in Ankara, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Wednesday.

title: US Court Approves Elon Musk's Settlement With Regulators In Twitter Probe
content: A federal judge on Wednesday approved the US Securities and Exchange Commission's settlement with Elon Musk over his purchase of Twitter shares.

title: Uttarakhand Becomes Country's 6th Fully Literate State
content: Uttarakhand on Wednesday became the country's sixth fully literate state under the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and the 'ULLAS' (Understanding Lifelong Learning for All in Society) programme, officials said.

title: US To Remove Syria From Terror Blacklist In Move To Boost Ties With Al-Sharaa
content: The United States said Wednesday it will delist Syria as a state sponsor of terrorism, a decades-old designation that severely impeded investment, in a new vote of confidence in leader Ahmed al-Sharaa.

title: US Court Rejects Trump Request To Restore His Name To Kennedy Center
content: The Trump administration and Kennedy Center board filed an appeal with the US Court of Appeals for the District of Colombia seeking a stay of Cooper's ruling.

title: 'Ready To Defend Every Inch Of NATO': Denmark After Trump's Greenland Remark
content: Trump's assertions that the US must acquire or control Greenland, a semi-autonomous Danish territory, have long strained relations between Washington and Copenhagen.

title: US launches major probe into H-1B visa 'fraud'; Cognizant under scanner
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Snakebite deaths much rarer than estimated: ICMR study
content: snakebite envenomation (SBE) was a disease of impoverishment with 53% of victims being below the poverty line . the mortality rate from snake bite is roughly 0.3 per 100,000 population, much lower than the earlier estimate of 6 per 100,000 .
